titleStmt.title: Letter from the Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland to the Registrar of Sir Patrick Dun's Hospital, 20 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Typescript letter stating that at RCPI has re-elected Dr. WH Thompson as King's Professor of Institute of Medicine in the School of Physics, and that 'the existing arrangements with regard to his deputy at St Patrick's Dun's Hospital will stand, and passing on regrets that the notice of Dr. Thompson's re-election was not sent before.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, Dublin

correspAction.persName: The Registrar of Sir Patrick Dun's Hospital
correspAction.placeName: The Registrar of Sir Patrick Dun's Hospital, Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
RCPI/2/3/2/13/152 20th MARCH 16 The Registrar,
Sir Patrick Dun's Hospital, Dublin. Dear Sir, I beg to inform you that at a special meeting of the
President and Fellows of the Royal College of Physicians of
Ireland held on Monday the 10th January,1916, Dr.W.H.Thompson
was re-elected King's Professor of Institutes of Medicine in
the School of Physic and presumably the existing arrangements
with regard to his deputy at Sir Patrick Dun's Hospital will
stand. I regret that owing to an oversight this formal notice
of Dr.Thompson's re-election was not conveyed to you before. I remain, dear Sir,
Yours faithfully, Fellow & Registrar.
